title: Citroen Berlingo
description: Citroen Berlingo Lease The Citroen Berlingo lease market offers an attractive path to driving this versatile vehicle without the burden of full ownership…
body:
Citroen Berlingo Lease The Citroen Berlingo lease market offers an attractive path to driving this versatile vehicle without the burden of full ownership costs. Whether you need a practical family…

mily car or a capable business vehicle, this multi-purpose van combines comfort, space, and efficiency in one affordable package.

Looking for the right lease deal can feel overwhelming with so many options available. From various contract lengths to different mileage allowances, each choice affects your monthly payments and overall experience.

While buying might seem like the obvious choice, leasing a Berlingo could actually save you money in the long run. You’ll get a brand-new vehicle with warranty coverage, predictable monthly costs, and the freedom to upgrade to a newer model when your contract ends.

This comprehensive guide breaks down everything you need to know about Citroen Berlingo leasing options, helping you find the best deal for your needs. We’ll explore key features, compare different lease packages, and examine the financial benefits of choosing a Berlingo lease over purchase.

## Citroen Berlingo: Key Features and Specifications

Designed with versatility in mind, the Citroen Berlingo presents an impressive array of features that make it a compelling option for those considering a vehicle lease. From powerful engines to spacious interiors, this multi-purpose vehicle delivers practicality without compromising on performance or comfort.

### Engine options and performance

The Berlingo offers several powertrain options to suit different driving preferences and needs. For petrol enthusiasts, the 1.2-liter three-cylinder PureTech 110 engine delivers 110hp and adequate acceleration with a 0-62mph time of 11.5 seconds and a top speed of 107mph 1. This engine provides sufficient power for everyday driving while maintaining fuel economy of up to 45.5 mpg 2.

Diesel options include the economical 1.5-liter BlueHDi, available in multiple power outputs. The BlueHDi 100 produces 102hp with impressive fuel efficiency reaching up to 54.8 mpg 2, making it ideal for high-mileage lessees. The more powerful BlueHDi 130 generates 131hp and delivers stronger torque of 300Nm 3, perfect for those who frequently carry heavy loads or passengers.

Furthermore, the all-electric ë-Berlingo features a 100kW (136hp) motor paired with a 50kWh/52kWh battery, offering a WLTP range of up to 213 miles 4 and rapid charging capability from 0-80% in just 30 minutes 4. This option may significantly reduce running costs during your lease period.

Transmission choices include a six-speed manual gearbox as standard, while the BlueHDi 130 is also available with an eight-speed automatic transmission labeled EAT8 1, providing smooth gear changes for a more relaxed driving experience.

### Cargo capacity and dimensions

The Berlingo comes in two lengths: M (4.40m) and XL (4.75m) 1, catering to different space requirements. The standard M version offers a generous load volume of 3.3 cubic meters, while the XL extends this to 3.9 cubic meters 5.

Load space is impressively versatile with the M version providing a maximum load length of 1,817mm and the XL offering 2,167mm 5. The width between wheelarches measures 1,230mm 5, wide enough to accommodate a Euro pallet widthways.

In addition, the innovative Modutop® roof incorporates a translucent arch, rear roof box, ambient lighting, and a panoramic glass roof with electric blinds 4, enhancing both practicality and comfort. With seats folded, total volume expands dramatically – up to 4,000 liters for the XL version 4.

### Technology and comfort features

The Berlingo boasts an impressive technological package centered around an 8-inch or 10-inch touchscreen infotainment system 6 that controls navigation, smartphone integration via Apple CarPlay and Android Auto, and vehicle settings. Higher trim levels include Citroen Connect navigation with voice recognition 1.

Comfort is prioritized with Citroen Advanced Comfort® seats – an exclusive feature in this segment 4. The cabin offers exceptional practicality with up to 27 storage compartments providing a total volume of up to 186 liters 4, including a top box glove compartment (made possible by relocating the passenger airbag to the roof) 3.

Safety technology includes:

-   Emergency Braking Assistance
-   Automatic intelligent beam headlights
-   Rear parking sensors
-   Electric parking brake
-   Cruise control with speed limiter 3

Higher trims add a 180° color reversing camera, blind spot monitoring, and front parking sensors 3, contributing to a safer leasing experience over the contract period.

### Available trim levels

The current Berlingo lineup offers two streamlined trim levels: Plus and Max 6. The Plus trim provides essential features including 16-inch steel wheels, manual air conditioning, a 10-inch HD touchscreen, and rear parking sensors 3.

The Max trim builds upon this foundation with 17-inch ‘Topaz’ alloy wheels, gloss black roof bars, a leather steering wheel, three independent rear seats, automatic air conditioning, and a 180° color reversing camera 3. This trim represents excellent value for those seeking more luxury throughout their lease term.

Previously, the range included Feel, Flair, and Flair XTR variants 2 alongside Enterprise Edition and Driver Edition options 1 designed for different usage patterns. While these older trim designations have been phased out, understanding their specifications remains helpful when considering pre-owned lease returns.

Each trim level affects the residual value calculation differently, consequently impacting monthly lease payments and making certain configurations more financially attractive for specific lease terms.

## Understanding Berlingo Lease Basics

Leasing a Citroen Berlingo presents a cost-effective alternative to outright purchase, allowing drivers to access this versatile vehicle through fixed monthly payments. Before diving into specific deals, understanding the fundamentals of vehicle leasing will help you make an informed decision about the right Berlingo lease for your needs.

### How vehicle leasing works

At its core, a Citroen Berlingo lease is an agreement that gives you use of the vehicle for a predetermined period without ownership. Instead of buying the van outright, you pay fixed monthly installments that essentially cover the vehicle’s depreciation during your use. Once your contract ends, you simply return the vehicle to the finance company 7.

The primary advantages of leasing include:

-   Lower monthly payments compared to purchasing
-   No concerns about depreciation or resale value
-   Fixed costs for easier budgeting
-   Option to drive a new vehicle every few years
-   Road tax included for the contract duration 8

Unlike Personal Contract Purchase (PCP), where you have the option to buy the vehicle at the end, a standard lease agreement requires you to return the Berlingo when the term concludes 8.

### Business vs. personal lease differences

Citroen Berlingo leases come in two main varieties: Business Contract Hire and Personal Contract Hire, each with distinct advantages depending on your circumstances.

Business Contract Hire starts from £395 per month (excluding VAT) plus initial rental 3. This option particularly benefits companies through potential tax advantages, as lease payments on vans used exclusively for business can often be claimed as business expenses 3. Additionally, all Leasys business contracts (18 months onwards) include roadside assistance, road fund license, and driver helpline at no additional cost 3.

Personal Contract Hire, alternatively, offers similar structural benefits but is designed for individual users. With Citroen Personal Contract Hire, you simply choose an initial rental and term length that suits your needs and return the vehicle when your contract ends 7. Personal leases include VAT in the stated monthly price, unlike business leases which quote figures excluding VAT 9.

### Contract length options

Flexibility remains a key advantage of Citroen Berlingo leasing, with contracts typically ranging from 24 to 60 months 7. Most providers offer standard terms of 24, 36, and 48 months, though some extend to 60 months for those seeking longer commitments and potentially lower monthly payments 10. Shorter terms are available with select providers, sometimes starting from just 12 months for those requiring greater flexibility 11.

### Initial payment considerations

The initial payment, often called the “initial rental,” significantly impacts your monthly lease costs. Most Berlingo lease agreements offer flexibility in this area, with options typically ranging from 1 to 12 months’ worth of payments upfront 12.

Higher initial payments generally result in lower monthly installments throughout the remainder of your contract. For instance, standard offers might feature an initial payment of £319 followed by 47 monthly payments at the same rate for personal contracts 7. Business users might see offers starting with a larger initial payment but reduced subsequent monthly costs.

Consequently, carefully balancing your initial outlay against monthly budget constraints represents an essential step in structuring your ideal Berlingo lease agreement. Additionally, be mindful that excess mileage and vehicle condition charges may apply if you return the vehicle in suboptimal condition 10.

## Exploring Citroen Berlingo Lease Packages

Alongside the vehicle itself, Citroen Berlingo lease packages include various components that enhance value and minimize unexpected costs. Understanding these elements helps potential lessees make informed decisions about which package best suits their needs and budget.

### Standard lease inclusions

When leasing a Citroen Berlingo, several essential components come standard in most agreements. Notably, all lease deals typically include road tax, breakdown cover, and the manufacturer’s warranty for the duration of your contract 13. Free mainland GB delivery represents another valuable inclusion, saving you the hassle of collecting your vehicle 13. Many providers also offer a fast lease service with delivery possible in as little as four weeks for in-stock vehicles 13.

For personal leases, standard packages often feature an initial rental followed by fixed monthly payments. Current offers start from around £297 per month for the Berlingo Estate with a standard 48-month term 5. The ë-Berlingo electric variant begins at approximately £346.89 monthly, reflecting its advanced powertrain technology 5.

### Maintenance package options

Subsequently, many lessees opt to enhance their standard package with maintenance options. Citroen offers a FLEXCARE service plan that covers scheduled servicing at a fixed price, making budgeting more predictable 14. Monthly costs start from £18.72 for petrol/diesel models and £10.81 for electric variants 14.

More comprehensive coverage comes with the Complete Care service and maintenance plan, which includes:

-   Scheduled servicing by trained technicians
-   Replacement of wear items (brake pads, wipers, shock absorbers)
-   Parts failure replacement after manufacturer warranty expiration
-   MOT inspections for plans over three years 14

Specifically for business users, maintenance packages covering service, maintenance, and tires are available as optional add-ons to standard contract hire agreements 3.

### Mileage allowance choices Citroen Berlingo Lease

Meanwhile, selecting an appropriate mileage allowance proves crucial when configuring your Berlingo lease. Most providers offer allowances starting from 5,000 miles annually 4, with options to increase based on your expected usage. Popular configurations include 8,000 and 10,000 miles per year, with corresponding price adjustments 5.

The mileage limit helps leasing companies accurately forecast the vehicle’s residual value 4. Nevertheless, excessive mileage would increase wear and tear, reducing the vehicle’s value at term end 4. Although unlimited mileage leases aren’t typically available, funders may permit up to 50,000 miles annually or 180,000 miles over the entire agreement for high-usage customers 4.

Carefully considering your driving habits before selecting a mileage allowance prevents potentially costly excess mileage charges when returning your Berlingo at contract end.

## Current Berlingo Lease Deals and Offers

The current marketplace offers numerous Citroen Berlingo lease deals with competitive rates for both business and personal users. With options spanning different model variants, contract lengths, and payment structures, finding the right offer requires comparing multiple providers to secure optimal value.

### Best business contract hire rates

Business users can access the Berlingo through competitive contract hire agreements starting from just £275.50 per month (excluding VAT) on a 48-month agreement with an initial payment of £2,725.33 8. For those seeking the latest models, the standard diesel Berlingo Panel Van M BlueHDi 100 with 6-speed manual transmission in Enterprise trim is available from £325 monthly plus VAT with an initial rental of £1,950 15.

The electric ë-Berlingo presents a sustainable alternative for businesses, with current offers from £329 per month 12 through to £395 per month plus VAT on a 36-month contract with 10,000 annual miles 3. Higher spec variants like the ë-Berlingo Panel Van M 54kWh Driver trim command £395 plus VAT monthly with a £2,370 initial payment 15.

### Personal lease special offers Citroen Berlingo Lease

For individual users, personal contract hire deals start from approximately £313.96 monthly for the 1.5 BlueHDi 100 Plus M model on a 48-month contract 1. The seven-seat XL variant costs from £318.90 per month 1, making it an affordable family transport solution.

Current personal lease promotions include deals from £319 monthly with matching £319 initial payment on the PureTech 110 model 7. Many personal lease deals include an 8,000-mile annual allowance as standard 8, with options to increase for additional cost.

### Seasonal promotions and incentives

Throughout the year, lease providers offer enhanced deals coinciding with registration plate changes in March and September 16. First quarter 2025 promotions include manufacturer deposit contributions of up to £989 on Personal Contract Purchase agreements 7, often convertible to lease incentives.

### Dealer-specific deals Citroen Berlingo Lease

Individual Citroen dealers frequently offer exclusive Berlingo lease packages beyond manufacturer promotions. Donnelly Group currently provides tailored business contract hire arrangements on both diesel and electric variants 15, while Select Car Leasing guarantees price matching on their advertised rates 1.

Many dealers offer flexible acquisition methods beyond standard leasing, including finance lease options from £349 per month plus VAT 3 and conditional sale arrangements for those preferring eventual ownership 7. Additionally, some dealers include priority service bookings for business users and Saturday appointment availability 3, enhancing the overall leasing experience.

## Financial Aspects of Leasing a Berlingo

Understanding the complete financial picture of a Citroen Berlingo lease extends beyond the headline monthly rate. The various components and obligations involved directly impact the overall value proposition of your leasing arrangement.

### Monthly payment breakdown Citroen Berlingo Lease

The monthly cost structure of a Citroen Berlingo lease typically begins with an initial rental payment, often equivalent to multiple months of the standard rate. Current offers include options starting from £319 initial rental followed by 47 monthly payments of the same amount 7. Personal Contract Hire agreements generally include an annual mileage allowance of 8,000 miles as standard 7.

For business users, monthly payments exclude VAT, whereas personal lease costs include VAT. These payments cover the vehicle’s depreciation during your contract period rather than building equity toward ownership.

### Tax benefits for businesses

Business users enjoy significant tax advantages when leasing a Berlingo. VAT-registered businesses can reclaim up to 50% of the VAT on car lease payments 6. Moreover, limited companies can offset their monthly rental costs against corporation tax 6, potentially claiming up to 100% of lease payments as business expenses 2.

For vans specifically, the tax treatment is even more favorable—regardless of emissions levels, 100% of the cost can be offset against business taxes 2. This creates substantial savings compared to outright purchase, where vehicle depreciation impacts your balance sheet.

### End-of-lease costs Citroen Berlingo Lease

At contract conclusion, you must return the Berlingo in good condition, subject to reasonable fair wear and tear 17. Professional cleaning is advisable before handover. Exceeding your agreed mileage limit triggers excess charges calculated on a pence-per-mile basis 18—currently around 6.9p per mile (excluding VAT) for business contracts 17 and 12.08p per mile (including VAT) for personal agreements 19.

### Early termination considerations

Should circumstances change, early termination options exist but typically incur substantial fees. Most finance providers charge approximately 50% of remaining rentals plus any arrears 18, though some may require all outstanding payments 20.

Termination is generally at the finance provider’s discretion and unavailable for Personal Contract Hire agreements unless £1,500 (including VAT) of rentals have already been paid 18. Administration fees and vehicle collection charges may apply in addition to the main termination fee 20.

## Conclusion Citroen Berlingo Lease

Choosing a Citroen Berlingo lease offers significant advantages for both business and personal users. The vehicle’s versatile design, coupled with multiple powertrain options and impressive cargo capacity, makes it an excellent choice for diverse needs. Business users benefit particularly from tax advantages, while personal lessees enjoy fixed monthly costs and warranty coverage throughout their contract term.

Though leasing costs start competitively, careful consideration of initial payments, mileage allowances, and maintenance packages remains essential for optimal value. The current market presents attractive deals across different configurations, especially for those ready to commit to longer terms or willing to pay higher initial rentals for reduced monthly payments.

Understanding end-of-lease obligations helps avoid unexpected charges when returning your Berlingo. Smart lessees factor in potential excess mileage fees and maintain their vehicles according to the agreed terms. This approach, combined with proper research into available packages and promotions, ensures a satisfying leasing experience while maximizing the financial benefits of choosing a Berlingo over traditional purchase options.
